The Vault v0.4.9 is a recall reliability release: memory retrieval now returns what you actually asked for.

Highlights:
- Fixes a candidate-filter bug that leaked every promoted memory from every project into every recall; recall results are now strictly scoped to the queried project.
- Resolves project slugs and casing variants (for example `vault-collab` or `DeutschBoost`) to the canonical project name across recall, find, and latest-item tools, matching the behavior of the save path.
- Ranks relevance-bearing matches above static-boost-only items whenever a query carries a subject, keywords, tags, or query text, so promoted and canonical memories can no longer bury an on-topic match; project-only session-start recalls keep their promoted-first briefing order.
- Stops the related-memory expansion step from re-sorting ranked results, preserving relevance ordering end to end.
- Adds regression test coverage for recall candidate scoping, project resolution, and ranking accuracy.

This build packages the desktop app and the bundled `vault-memory` MCP sidecar runtime. After installing, open The Vault and use Settings -> Client setup to connect Codex, Claude Desktop, or Claude Code.
